title: Phragmipedium × conchiferum
taxonomy: Phragmipedium × conchiferum is a hybrid orchid species classified within the genus Phragmipedium. It is a member of the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This species is further categorized as a hybrid of P. lindleyanum and P. lusiae, combining characteristics from both parent species. As a result, Phragmipedium × conchiferum exhibits unique traits while maintaining its place within the orchid family. Its classification reflects its hybrid origin and evolutionary relationships with other Phragmipedium species.

intro: Phragmipedium × conchiferum, also known as the Conchiferum, is a hybrid species of orchid, specifically a cross between Phragmipedium andlinguifolium and Phragmipedium andeanum. This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Phragmipedium × conchiferum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are infusions, tinctures, and extracts, often used in traditional medicine to support respiratory health and alleviate inflammation.
